result.recit:
## Opening of File — Méridian Studios v. Cassiopée/Julien Mercier (Tohu-Bohu)

---

### 📍 Where Things Stand

**Score 57/100 · Distance to goal: 26 points · 7/9 blocks established · […] irreversible: August 3, 2026 — 39 days.**

We are in a posture of temporal urgency. The five-year limitations period for the copyright infringement action (L. 335-1 CPI + art. 2224 C. civ.) expires in 39 days. The critical path is: petition to the TJ → ex parte order → enforcement by the bailiff (D+7) → summons on the merits (D+36). We are within the window, but it is closing. Every day lost is one fewer day to cure a procedural irregularity.

---

### 🔧 The LEVER: admissibility (7.2/20 — the weakest dimension)

This is where Cassiopée will strike in […], and this is where the file is most exposed. Two sub-vulnerabilities:

**1. Attribution of Tohu-Bohu to Cassiopée/Julien Mercier — fragile block, vulnerability not closed.**
There is not yet a certified capture of the legal notices of Tohu-Bohu on Carto Royale 2 or […] Play designating Cassiopée or Julien Mercier as publisher/developer. Cassiopée and Julien Mercier deny any connection with the application. Without this exhibit, the file collapses on the attribution issue before even reaching the merits. The […] act closes the vulnerability: **bailiff's report on the […]**, certifying the public developer profile. To be carried out […], before any enforcement of the infringement seizure (saisie-contrefaçon) — and before Cassiopée withdraws the application or modifies the legal notices.

**2. Méridian Studios' standing to sue — solid but needs to be ironclad.**
Cassiopée will challenge the assignment […] → Méridian Studios under L. 131-3 (absence of specification of rights assigned, duration, territory). The counter-argument is robust: the presumption under L. 113-1 does not require a deed of assignment — it arises from acts of public, undisturbed, and unequivocal exploitation under the name Méridian Studios since 2018 (Exhibit 6 Bis Amazon, Kbis, trademark no. 4449595, bailiff's reports […]). It is for Cassiopée to rebut this presumption by affirmative proof. The email […] of 09/01/2019 constitutes at minimum a commencement of written proof. But this argument must be articulated from the outset in the summons (assignation), not held in reserve.

---

### ⚡ The Next Most Profitable Move: saisie-contrefaçon petition + simultaneous […] bailiff's report

This is the move to make now, for three reasons […] that are cumulative:

**It beats the […]** The petition to the […] of the TJ triggers the only procedural path […] that results in a prescription-interrupting summons before August 3. There is no alternative.

**It closes the attribution vulnerability.** The bailiff's report on the […] (legal notices of Tohu-Bohu = Cassiopée/Julien Mercier) is the act […] that closes the attribution block. Without it, Cassiopée denies any connection with Tohu-Bohu and the file loses its defendant.

**It secures evidence before it disappears.** A mobile application can be withdrawn from the […] or its legal notices modified within 24 hours. Every day without a bailiff's report is a risk of irreversible loss of evidence.

**The opposing threat regarding the seizure is parriable**, but it is real: Cassiopée has two decisions at hand — Adverse Exhibit 11 (TGI Paris, April 4, 2014, nullity for failure to serve a separate service of process) and Adverse Exhibit 18 (CA Aix-en-Provence, September 15, 2016, nullity due to unauthorized presence of the petitioner). The counter-measure is technical but imperative: draft the petition authorizing only the bailiff and the designated expert, with no representative of Méridian Studios present; produce a separate act of service of the order prior to the commencement of operations; ensure that the minutes (procès-verbal) record no unsolicited statements not authorized by the order. These conditions being met, the Cass. civ. 1re decisions of 2015 validate the regularity of the procedure.

---

### 🔄 What […] Has Moved Recently

The engine advanced 10 blocks during the refresh of June 25, 2026. The […] does not detail the content of the advances — it is a priority to verify whether the **attribution** block has changed status or remains "alleged/fragile." This is the most sensitive point: if this block has not moved, the […] bailiff's report is urgency no. 1, even before the petition.

---

### 🔍 What You May Not Have Looked At

**1. The trademark infringement theory — candidate at 57, not yet […]**
The Méridian Studios trademark no. 4449595 is in the file. If Tohu-Bohu uses the name "Bastille" or similar signs on the […] or in its communications, this constitutes an independent ground — and is strategically stronger than copyright on one specific point: there is no debate on originality. The test is that of likelihood of confusion (L. 713-2 and L. 713-3 CPI), which is more binary. To be analyzed before the summons in order to plead multiple grounds.

**2. The analytical originality memorandum — missing exhibit, payoff 9 (the highest).**
This is the most profitable move on the merits. Exhibit 17 demonstrates that the disputed formulations were circulating online prior to Cassiopée — they fall within the public domain. But Cassiopée relies on Exhibit 23 (comparative table of Tempo/Tohu-Bohu card titles) to support the claim of copying. Without a detailed comparative analytical memorandum setting out the specific creative choices of Bastille (formulation of challenges, category architecture, visual identity) and distinguishing them point by point from competing games, the **Originality (pivot) & reproduction** block remains exposed. This is the exhibit […] that responds to the "public domain" threat and […] neutralizes Adverse Exhibit 23. It must be produced before the […] merits briefs. Rely on Cass. civ. 1re, Sept. 30, 2015, no. 14-11.944 (methods of establishing originality) and CA Paris (Exhibit 14) for the legal framework.

**3. The contradiction between Exhibit 17 and Exhibit 23 — not yet exploited.**
Exhibit 17 (online formulations predating Cassiopée) directly contests Exhibit 23 (Cassiopée's comparative table). This contradiction is an offensive weapon: if the card titles claimed by Cassiopée were already circulating […] online, Cassiopée cannot claim them as original. The court cannot supply Cassiopée's failure to establish originality (TJ Paris, 12/18/2020, Exhibit 12; TGI Paris, 09/29/2016, Exhibit 22). This line of attack must appear explicitly in the summons.

**4. The limitations period: the starting point must be secured against the "independent triggering event" argument.**
Cassiopée may argue that each act of exploitation of Tohu-Bohu constitutes an independent and continuing triggering event, pushing back the starting point of the limitations period. The counter-argument: demonstrate that Cassiopée, a professional actor in the board game sector, necessarily had knowledge of Tohu-Bohu from the date of its public release — a vigilant competitor cannot be unaware of a competing application accessible on the […]. This exhibit (dated bailiff's report of […] Tohu-Bohu's release date) is **not yet covered** in the file. To be obtained by bailiff's report or through the public metadata of the […].

**5. Art. 145 CPC held in reserve.**
If the developer profile of Tohu-Bohu is masked or if the legal notices do not directly identify Cassiopée/Julien Mercier, a petition under art. 145 CPC (pre-litigation discovery order) must be anticipated to obtain disclosure of the developer account registration data from Apple and […]. This pre-litigation evidentiary measure (mesure d'instruction in futurum) may be sought in parallel with the saisie-contrefaçon. To be prepared now, not after the bailiff's report fails.

---

### 🎬 The 4 Moves to Trigger

| # | Action | Why Now |
|---|--------|---------|
| 🚨 1 | **Saisie-contrefaçon (infringement seizure) petition to the TJ** | Beats the […] of August 3 — irreversible if not done |
| 🔍 2 | **Analyze the Attribution block / carry out […] bailiff's report

suggestions.label: 🚨 File the saisie-contrefaçon (infringement seizure) petition (TJ)
suggestions.reason: Deadline of […] August 3, 2026 (39 days): triggers the chain petition → order → execution → assignation (summons). Without this act, the statute of limitations expires and the cause of action is irreversibly lost.

suggestions.label: 🔍 Analyze the Imputability block (close the […] gap)
suggestions.reason: Fragile block = fatal flaw: absent a certified record of the Tohu-Bohu legal notices on Carto Royale 2/[…] Play designating Cassiopée/Julien Mercier, the opposing party will deny any connection to the application. The closing act is the bailiff's process-server report […] — to be initiated before execution of the infringement seizure.

suggestions.label: 📝 Draft the analytical originality memorandum (payoff 9)
suggestions.reason: Highest-value substantive move (payoff 9): the comparative memorandum Bastille v. third-party games closes the Originality block (pivot) & reproduction, neutralizes opposing Exhibit 23, and answers the 'common stock' threat. Without it, Cassiopée prevails on originality.

suggestions.label: ⚔️ Red-team: stress-test the saisie-contrefaçon (infringement seizure) nullity threat
suggestions.reason: Cassiopée holds two decisions (opposing Exhibit 11, TGI Paris 2014 + opposing Exhibit 18, CA Aix 2016) to void the infringement-seizure report (procès-verbal de saisie). The red-team exercise identifies the vulnerabilities in the petition prior to filing — a nullity of the seizure would be catastrophic with 39 days remaining before […].
